title: CLI /send: inner 'continue' on chunk error does not break outer loop
description: In commands.rs /send handler, the chunk loop is a for loop inside the outer tokio::select loop. When MLS encrypt or pad fails on a chunk, the code uses 'continue' which continues the inner for loop (skipping that chunk), not the outer select loop. Subsequent chunks are still sent with a gap. The receiver assembles a file with None slots at the failed positions, fails the SHA-256 check, and discards the whole transfer. The sender shows 'pad failed for chunk N' but the transfer nominally continues. Trigger: MLS encryption failure mid-transfer. Bad outcome: partial transfer attempted, silently discarded on receiver, no abort notification.
